title: agrostis capillaris var. pumila
intro: Agrostis capillaris var. pumila, also known as dwarf red fescue, is a small, low-growing grass species that belongs to the family Poaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high content of essential oils and phenolic compounds. The health benefits of Agrostis capillaris var. pumila include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce inflammation, and act as a natural antiseptic, making it a popular herbal remedy for wounds, cuts, and skin conditions. The plant is typically prepared for medicinal use through herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and topical applications, which can be made by drying and grinding the leaves and stems of the plant.
